skill.name: writing-plans
skill.description: Create detailed implementation plans for multi-step engineering work before code changes begin.
skill.summary: Guides agents to write clear, task-based implementation plans with files, tests, commands, and handoff options.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 16 external command patterns and 3 weak cryptography patterns. Review found the command hits are Markdown examples or inline paths, and the weak cryptography hits are prose or template text rather than cryptographic code. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, network access, or executable script behavior was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Command Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported external command locations are Markdown fences, inline file paths, or example commands for generated implementation plans. The skill does not execute shell commands or call a Ruby backtick operator.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review shows these locations are documentation examples inside SKILL.md. No executable script, interpreter call, or command-dispatch mechanism is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Detector
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported weak cryptography locations are plain English description text or template placeholder text. No hashing, encryption, password storage, or cryptographic API usage appears in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose such as the skill description, assumptions about developers, and a goal placeholder. They do not contain algorithm names or cryptographic implementation code.

content.value_statement: Complex engineering tasks often fail when scope, files, tests, and handoffs are unclear. This skill turns requirements into practical implementation plans with small tasks, test steps, and execution options.
actual_capabilities: Creates structured implementation plans from specifications or requirements.
Breaks work into small tasks with files, tests, commands, and expected results.
Encourages test-first development with failing tests before implementation steps.
Defines plan headers with goal, architecture, and technology stack sections.
Adds handoff options for subagent-driven or separate-session execution.
limitations: Does not implement code changes by itself.
Requires accurate project context to name exact files and commands.
May produce generic steps when requirements are vague or incomplete.
References related skills that may not exist in every environment.

use_cases.title: Plan a Feature Build
use_cases.description: Convert a feature specification into a task-by-task implementation plan with test checkpoints and commit points.

use_cases.title: Prepare Engineering Handoff
use_cases.description: Create a plan that another agent or developer can follow without prior repository context.

use_cases.title: Structure TDD Work
use_cases.description: Define failing tests, minimal implementation steps, passing checks, and review gates before coding begins.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Feature Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a short feature description.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an implementation plan for this feature: [describe feature]. Include files to inspect, tests to add, implementation steps, and verification commands.

prompt_templates.title: Bug Fix Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a known defect.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a step-by-step fix plan for this bug: [describe bug]. Include reproduction steps, likely files, failing tests, minimal fix steps, and final validation.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request that needs sequencing and risk control.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an implementation plan for refactoring [component or workflow]. Preserve behavior, identify test coverage first, split the work into safe tasks, and include rollback checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Team Handoff Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for another developer or agent.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete handoff plan for [project goal]. Assume the implementer has no repository context. Include exact files, task order, test strategy, expected command results, and execution options.

output_examples.input: Plan a new user settings page with validation and tests.
output_examples.output: A plan with a goal, architecture summary, task list, files to create or modify, failing test steps, implementation steps, verification commands, and commit checkpoints.

output_examples.input: Plan a fix for search results returning stale data.
output_examples.output: A debugging and repair plan that starts with reproduction, adds a targeted failing test, updates the cache behavior, verifies fresh results, and documents final checks.

output_examples.input: Plan a payment workflow refactor without changing behavior.
output_examples.output: A staged refactor plan with baseline tests, small behavior-preserving changes, review points, validation commands, and a clear execution handoff.

best_practices: Provide the current specification, known constraints, and repository context before asking for a plan.
Ask for exact files, test names, commands, expected results, and commit boundaries.
Review the generated plan before execution, especially when it references commands or related skills.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill as a substitute for reading the relevant codebase context.
Do not accept vague tasks that omit files, tests, or verification steps.
Do not execute generated commands without reviewing them for the current environment.

faq.question: Does this skill write code?
faq.answer: No. It writes implementation plans that guide later code changes and verification work.

faq.question: What input works best?
faq.answer: A clear specification, acceptance criteria, known files, constraints, and preferred test commands work best.

faq.question: Can it support test-driven development?
faq.answer: Yes. It explicitly structures tasks around failing tests, minimal implementation, passing tests, and commits.

faq.question: Does it run shell commands?
faq.answer: No. It may include example commands in plans, but it does not execute them.

faq.question: Can it create handoffs for other agents?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes execution handoff choices for same-session subagents or separate implementation sessions.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found only documentation examples and no executable code, network access, or data exfiltration behavior.
